fundamentals.name: MongoDB, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: MongoDB, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides general purpose database platform worldwide. The company offers MongoDB Atlas, a hosted multi-cloud database-as-a-service solution; MongoDB Enterprise Advanced, a commercial database server for enterprise customers to run in the cloud, on-premises, or in a hybrid environment; and Community Server, a free-to-download version of its database, which includes the functionality that developers need to get started with MongoDB. It offers professional services comprising consulting and training. The company was formerly known as 10gen, Inc. and changed its name to MongoDB, Inc. in August 2013. MongoDB, Inc. was incorporated in 2007 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: Fast-growing software company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable on a net basis and trading at a high forward multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 12.1%; next earnings date is 2026-05-28.
positives: Revenue growth 26.7% YoY, exceeding analyst estimates for the current fiscal year.
Free cash flow of $463.32M TTM indicates strong cash generation capabilities.
Gross margin of 71.7% is healthy and typical for software companies.
Consensus analyst rating is 'buy' with a mean target price of $358.82.
negatives: Profit margin is negative at -2.9%, and operating margin is 0.0%, indicating unprofitability.
Forward P/E of 44.81x and PEG ratio of 1.67x suggest a high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 2.13x is elevated, though offset by substantial cash reserves.
Quarterly earnings growth has recently turned negative (-1.9%).

news.paragraph: MongoDB's Instruqt platform was highlighted by Google Cloud Security at Google Next 2026 for training practitioners on Agentic AI, suggesting strong adoption and integration within key cloud ecosystems. This follows recent commentary noting analyst optimism and AI momentum supporting the stock's significant one-year rally. With the next earnings report due May 28th, the market will be looking for continued growth and profitability to justify the current valuation.
